questions.question: What is the primary purpose of connecting Mail to a Custom Agent?

options.text: To automatically draft and send emails without any user input
options.explanation: While agents can send emails, the primary purpose is to automate workflows, allowing them to read, organize, draft, and send emails on your behalf.

options.text: To connect Mail to a Custom Agent
options.explanation: The article states that Custom Agents can connect to Mail to automate email workflows, such as reading, organizing, drafting, and sending emails.

options.text: To replace your email provider entirely
options.explanation: Mail connections work with Gmail or Notion Mail, but they do not replace your email provider; they integrate with them to automate tasks.

options.text: To block all incoming emails from your inbox
options.explanation: Agents can block specific senders, but the primary purpose of the connection is to manage and automate email workflows, not block everything.

questions.question: Which of the following actions is supported by the Mail connection for Custom Agents?

options.text: Encrypt emails before sending
options.explanation: Encryption is not listed as a supported action. The supported actions include Search, Archive, Star, Trash, Manage labels, Unsubscribe, Block and unblock sender, Write, Send, and Manage filters.

options.text: Archive emails to clean up your inbox
options.explanation: The 'Archive' action is explicitly listed in the 'What your Custom Agent can do with Mail' table as a supported feature.

options.text: Set up email forwarding rules
options.explanation: While agents can manage filters, the article does not list 'forwarding rules' as a specific supported action.

options.text: Automatically reply to all emails without review
options.explanation: Agents can draft replies, but the article mentions saving drafts for review as a best practice for auto-reply assistants.

questions.question: Which of the following actions is ONLY available if you are using Notion Mail?

options.text: Block and unblock sender
options.explanation: Blocking/unblocking senders is supported in both Gmail and Notion Mail.

options.text: Manage email status and triage state
options.explanation: The 'Manage email status' action is listed specifically under the 'Notion Mail-only actions' section.

options.text: Write and send emails
options.explanation: Writing and sending emails are supported in both Gmail and Notion Mail.

options.text: Create reminders to follow up later
options.explanation: Creating reminders is a Notion Mail-only action, but the question asks for an action available in *both*.

questions.question: When setting up a Mail connection, what does the 'Modify inbox' permission allow the agent to do?

options.text: Only read emails without changing them
options.explanation: Modify inbox permission allows for specific actions like archiving, trashing, applying labels, setting reminders, blocking senders, and unsubscribing.

options.text: Archive, trash, apply labels, set reminders, block senders, and unsubscribe
options.explanation: This is the exact list of capabilities granted by the 'Modify inbox' permission as defined in the 'Mail permissions in Custom Agent' section.

options.text: Create new drafts and send emails
options.explanation: Creating drafts is handled by the 'Draft' permission, and sending by the 'Send' permission.

options.text: Access all emails in your connected accounts
options.explanation: Access is granted based on the accounts you explicitly connect and select, but 'Modify inbox' specifically refers to manipulating the state of those emails.

questions.question: How can you narrow down which specific emails trigger a Mail-based event?

options.text: By filtering for emails with attachments
options.explanation: You can filter by From, To, Subject, Body, or Domain, but not specifically by attachments.

options.text: By applying a specific label to the email
options.explanation: A label-based trigger fires when a label is applied, but you cannot filter the *trigger itself* by label; you select the label trigger type to begin with.

options.text: By adding a filter to narrow which events fire a Mail trigger
options.explanation: The article states that you can add a filter to narrow events to specific From, To, Subject, Body, or Domain attributes.

options.text: By setting the Agent's status to 'Active'
options.explanation: Agent status does not determine which emails trigger events; the trigger configuration and filters do.

questions.question: Can a Custom Agent send emails without your approval?

options.text: Yes, always
options.explanation: It depends on your settings. If you toggle 'Require confirmation' off, the agent can act without approval.

options.text: No, the agent can never send emails without approval
options.explanation: This is incorrect. If you give the agent the 'Send' permission and do not require confirmation, it can send emails without approval.

options.text: Yes, if you give it the 'Send' permission and include sending in your instructions
options.explanation: The FAQ confirms that an agent can send without approval if it has the 'send' permission and the instruction to send.

options.text: Only if you are using Notion Mail
options.explanation: This capability is available regardless of the email provider (Gmail or Notion Mail) as long as the permissions and instructions are set correctly.

questions.question: Which trigger type runs when a specific label is applied to an email?

options.text: New email received or sent
options.explanation: This trigger runs on any new email activity, not specifically when a label is applied.

options.text: Label applied to email
options.explanation: This is the specific trigger type listed for running when a label is applied to an email.

options.text: New email sent
options.explanation: This trigger only runs when *you* send an email, not when a label is applied.

options.text: New email received
options.explanation: This trigger runs when a new email arrives in the mailbox, regardless of whether it has a specific label.

questions.question: What is the recommended first step if your Custom Agent encounters errors?

options.text: Delete the connection and start over
options.explanation: The troubleshooting section suggests giving the run a 'thumbs down' and chatting with the Agent to diagnose the issue before restarting.

options.text: Contact Notion support immediately
options.explanation: Before contacting support, the article advises giving the run a thumbs down and chatting with the agent to try and diagnose the issue yourself.

options.text: Give the run a 'thumbs down' and chat with the Agent
options.explanation: The Troubleshooting section explicitly states: 'If your Custom Agent encounters errors, give the run a “thumbs down” and chat with the Agent to diagnose the issue.'

options.text: Change the Agent's permissions to Full access
options.explanation: While verifying permissions is part of the troubleshooting steps, giving the run a thumbs down and chatting is the very first recommended step.

questions.question: Which email providers are supported for the Mail connection?

options.text: Outlook and Yahoo Mail
options.explanation: The article states that Mail connections work with Gmail or Notion Mail, not Outlook or Yahoo Mail.

options.text: Gmail or Notion Mail
options.explanation: The article explicitly states that Mail connections work with Gmail or Notion Mail.

options.text: Apple Mail only
options.explanation: Apple Mail is not listed as a supported provider.

options.text: Any email provider with an IMAP connection
options.explanation: The article specifies Gmail and Notion Mail as the supported providers.

questions.question: Where do you go to add a new Mail connection to a Custom Agent?

options.text: Go to Settings → Tools & Access → + Add connection → Mail
options.explanation: The 'How to connect' section outlines this exact path: Go to Settings → Tools & Access → Choose + Add connection → Mail.

options.text: Go to Triggers → + Add trigger → Mail
options.explanation: You go to Triggers to set up *Mail triggers*, but you add the *connection* via Tools & Access.

options.text: Go to the Agent's Chat interface only
options.explanation: While you can chat with the agent, the article specifies that you must go to Settings → Tools & Access to add the connection.

options.text: Go to the Shared settings of the Agent
options.explanation: The connection is managed under the agent's own Settings, specifically Tools & Access.

questions.question: If you share a Custom Agent that is connected to Mail, who can view the emails in the connected inbox?

options.text: Only the administrator of the workspace
options.explanation: Anyone who has access to the Agent can view the emails in the connected inbox.

options.text: No one, the connection is private by default
options.explanation: The connection allows anyone with access to the Agent to view emails in the inbox.

options.text: Anyone who has access to that Agent
options.explanation: The article states that if you share a Custom Agent connected to Mail, anyone with access to that Agent can view emails in the connected inbox.

options.text: Only the user who created the connection
options.explanation: Sharing the agent grants access to the connection's data to anyone with agent access, not just the creator.

questions.question: Can a Custom Agent manage multiple Gmail addresses simultaneously?

options.text: No, an agent can only connect to one email account
options.explanation: The FAQ section explicitly states: 'Yes. You can connect more than one Gmail address with your agent.'

options.text: Yes, by connecting them one by one in the Notion Mail UI
options.explanation: The article confirms you can connect more than one Gmail address via the agent's Settings under Tools & Access.

options.text: Only if you use Notion Mail instead of Gmail
options.explanation: Multiple addresses can be managed regardless of whether you use Notion Mail or Gmail.

options.text: Yes, but only for reading emails, not sending them
options.explanation: Multiple addresses can be used for all supported actions, including sending.
